# 11. Google Search spam update

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


## Domain

Search / SEO

## Evidence type

Platform policy

## What the case demonstrates

Scaled content, site reputation abuse, and expired-domain abuse manipulate search relevance/usefulness.

## What is known

Google targeted these categories in March 2024 and later said the rollout reduced low-quality unoriginal results by 45%.

## What is not known / caution

Do not overstate beyond the source record. Distinguish documented enforcement, platform policy, reputable reporting, and unresolved allegations.

## Signal layers involved

- Visibility: whether the object appears widely seen or discussed.
- Credibility: whether observers infer independent validation.
- Conversion: whether apparent interest turns into sales, streams, installs, or signups.
- Attribution: whether the correct actor gets credit.
- Legitimacy: whether the signal becomes status or institutional trust.

## Ethical problem

The ethical issue is not simply that marketing exists. It is that the audience, platform, or market may infer genuine demand from signals whose origin, independence, or quality has been distorted.

## Evidence strength

Strong platform source.

## Slide use

Use this case to show a concrete version of the thesis: a proxy signal was used as evidence, but the path by which that signal was produced complicates or corrupts the inference.

## Source notes

- **Google Search March 2024 update / spam policies** (Platform policy / primary). Targets scaled content abuse, site reputation abuse, and expired domain abuse. URL: https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2024/03/core-update-spam-policies
- **Google Search update results — 45% less low-quality unoriginal content** (Platform blog / primary). Google said its March 2024 rollout produced 45% less low-quality, unoriginal content in Search results. URL: https://blog.google/products/search/google-search-update-march-2024/
